MINI MINUTES: The following matters were called together:
1) PTR-12-296277, Edward Hee & Bernadette Hee Trust; and
2) PTR-20-303887, Bernadette Woo Hee Living Trust.

Appearances:
Edward Koplowitz appeared for petitioner Linda Hee in the Bernadette Living Trust matter (via Zoom).
Lynn Searle appeared for petitioner Linda Hee in the Edward & Bernadette Hee Trust matter (via Zoom).
Ciaran O'Sullivan appeared for respondent Brian Hee in the Edward & Bernadatte Hee Trust matter.
Bruce Feder appeared for trustee Gloria Kwei in the Bernadette Hee Trust matter (via Zoom).
John Minton appeared for beneficiaries Alessandra Hee, Arianna Hee and Isabella Hee in the Bernadette Hee Trust matter (via Zoom).
Lillian Ng appeared to report status regarding accountings (via Zoom).

The Court inquired as to the status of the case. Counsel reported that the parties had scheduled a mediation session in both matters for January 28, 2026 with Judge Adams. Mr. Koplowitz stated that the parties may schedule a 2nd mediation session with Judge Adams if necessary. He requested the issuance of a Breslin order to ensure that any settlement reached at mediation would be binding on all interested parties. The Court agreed to issue a Breslin order covering both matters.

Mr. Koplowitz also requested tolling of the five-year statute to ensure parties would not be prejudiced by participating in mediation. Mr. O'Sullivan opposed tolling, and argued that the Court lacked authority to order tolling absent party stipulation. The Court declined to enter a tolling order.

Following discussion, the Court continued both matters and set a status hearing on mediation for February 11, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Ms. Ng confirmed an accounting for the Edward Hee Trust was submitted to the parties on October 22, 2025, covering May 2014 to 2022. She stated that work was ongoing to complete the 2022-2025 accounting and anticipated completion in early January 2026.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Off Calendar Mar-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lynn Searle for and with Linda Hee.
Bradley Kass for Brian Hee (Respondent).

Mr. Kass stated that the money from the sale of the properties is not for the purpose of doing repairs or reimbursement, that his Client gets a 75% credit against the settlement of $854,000.00 and that he has no problem with sale of the properties as long as the proceeds are held until the credits are figured out. Ms. Searle stated that there is danger to the residents, that there are issues (mold, sewage, structural problems) with the six unit property, expressed concern about the health and safety of the residents, and stated that her Client has a duty to the residents. Ms. Searle also stated that there is a huge sink whole, that there were leaks for years that undermined the area beneath the building, that the condition of the building was not known to her Client at the time of settlement, that the settlement agreement does not bar their right to sell the building and that they are not selling the property that needs repairs but they are selling other properties in general. In addition, Ms. Searle stated that there are no appraisals of the Vallejo properties, that there is no cooperation from Ms. Kass' Client and that there is an emergency/real danger of the building collapsing. The Court suggested the parties go back to the Mediator, stated that the issue of reimbursement of the $111,000.00 needs to be handled in a separate petition, that it will authorize sale, but not where the money is spent, and directed the parties to work on selection of a Realtor. The parties indicated that they have no issue with the Bank of America account. The Court granted the Petition for Authority to Sell Real Property, etc. (subject to receipt and review of the order). The order is to be circulated for approval as to form.
